video:video.video:title: Video shows oil spill in Iranian island after attack on a refinery last month
video:video.video:description: Mobile phone footage shot April 9 by an Iranian named Ehsan Jalali shows thick black smoke rising after the strike on an oil refinery on Lavan, an island just off mainland Iran near Shidvar.

video:video.video:title: Kylie on early career press interactions
video:video.video:description: Pop star Kylie Minogue says viewers of her documentary "KYLIE" will be surprised by the questions she had to deal with in her youth.

video:video.video:title: More than 17,000 under evacuation orders as Southern California wildfire threatens homes
video:video.video:description: Thousands were under evacuation orders on Tuesday as a wind-driven wildfire threatened suburban neighborhoods in Southern California. (AP video shot by Caroline Brehman)

video:video.video:title: 'The Boys' wraps up in LA
video:video.video:description: Daveed Diggs and the cast of "The Boys" reunite in Los Angeles to celebrate the final season of the hit superhero show.

video:video.video:title: US test fires mobile rocket system near Mt Fuji in rapid 'shoot and scoot' drill
video:video.video:description: U.S. Marines test fired a dozen rockets from a mobile launcher on Wednesday at a range in the foothills of Japan's iconic Mount Fuji, in an exercise to keep sharp on a weapon that is a growingly important component of the American military's arsenal.

video:video.video:title: Police chief praises 'heroic actions' of security guard who died in San Diego mosque shooting
video:video.video:description: Police raced Monday to catch an armed teenage runaway before he and another teen opened fire on a San Diego mosque, killing three men and then themselves.

video:video.video:title: WHO says risk of global spread of Ebola outbreak is low, but high at national, regional levels
video:video.video:description: The new outbreak of a rare Ebola type in Congo and Uganda poses low global risk, the World Health Organization said Wednesday, but responders on the ground described being underprotected and undertrained for a rapidly spreading virus with no approved medicine or vaccine.

video:video.video:title: Preocupa la magnitud y la rapidez de la propagación del ébola mientras el Congo anuncia 134 muertes
video:video.video:description: El director general de la Organización Mundial de la Salud expresó su preocupación el martes por la magnitud del brote de ébola en la República Democrática del Congo.

video:video.video:title: Trump’s portrait hits New Delhi traffic as US Embassy rolls out 'Happy Birthday America!' rickshaws
video:video.video:description: About 100 auto-rickshaws carrying large images of Trump and the Statue of Liberty have appeared across the Indian capital in recent weeks.

video:video.video:title: 'My dear friend': Putin greets Xi at bilateral talks in China
video:video.video:description: Chinese leader Xi Jinping held a bilateral meeting with Russian President Vladimir Putin in Beijing on Wednesday to reaffirm ties.

video:video.video:title: How did Hoyeon prepare for her role in 'Hope'?
video:video.video:description: Hoyeon says she loved being challenged by her role in "Hope," learning to drive a manual car and shooting a firearm

video:video.video:title: Cannes jury immortalized at handprint ceremony
video:video.video:description: Demi Moore, Ruth Negga, Stellan Skarsgård, Chloé Zhao, Park Chan-wook and the rest of the Cannes jury have their legacy cemented at the festival's annual handprint ceremony.

video:video.video:title: Unexploded drone removed from Odesa apartment building
video:video.video:description: Unexploded drone removed from Odesa apartment building

video:video.video:title: Landmark 'Brady Bunch' home opens to fans with set replicas and Easter eggs
video:video.video:description: Fans of "The Brady Bunch" can now walk through their TV memories inside the landmark Los Angeles home used for exterior shots on the hit 1970s sitcom.

video:video.video:title: Lithuanian lawmakers rush to shelter as drone alarm underlines NATO's eastern jitters
video:video.video:description: Lithuanian lawmakers rushed to shelter because of an alarm over drone activity near the border with Belarus.

video:video.video:title: George Clooney, Jon Hamm, others looks back on the legacy of Stephen Colbert
video:video.video:description: As Stephen Colbert prepares to say goodbye to "The Late Show with Stephen Colbert" Thursday night, former guests and famous colleagues reflect on the impact he has made.

video:video.video:title: UN considers response to Israeli move to build military compound on Palestinian relief agency site
video:video.video:description: Israeli authorities seized control of the United Nations Relief and Works Agency’s offices in Sheikh Jarrah, East Jerusalem and began bulldozing parts of the premises in January.

video:video.video:title: Flower Power exhibit at NYC botanic garden explores psychedelic era environmental awakening
video:video.video:description: The New York Botanical Garden’s summer exhibit explores the cultural and environmental impact of 1960s flower power. (AP Video: Ted Shaffrey)

video:video.video:title: Rep. Thomas Massie concedes defeat to Ed Gallrein after losing Kentucky GOP primary
video:video.video:description: Ed Gallrein won Republican nomination for U.S. House in Kentucky’s 4th Congressional District, beating incumbent Thomas Massie.

video:video.video:title: Eight people injured in Russian attack on Sumy, Ukraine's emergency service says
video:video.video:description: Eight people were injured as a result of a Russian strike on Sumy, the state emergency service said.

video:video.video:title: An American citizen who contracted Ebola in Africa arrives in Berlin for treatment
video:video.video:description: Special emergency vehicles transported an American citizen who contracted Ebola in Africa to a specialized clinic in Berlin, officials said.

video:video.video:title: Plastic bags don't go in the recycling bin. What should you do instead?
video:video.video:description: Recycling plastic grocery bags can jam up machinery, but there are other ways to get rid of them.

video:video.video:title: 2026's most endangered historic places nod to America 250 and the promise of equality for all
video:video.video:description: The Stonewall National Monument, the President's House Site, and the Women's Rights National Historic Park are on a list of the 11 most endangered historic places in the U.S. compiled by the National Trust for Historic Preservation.

video:video.video:title: Residents of Lithuania's capital told to shelter as drone alarm underlines NATO's eastern jitters
video:video.video:description: Residents of Vilnius were told to take shelter, and airspace over Vilnius Airport was closed for about an hour on Wednesday after a suspected drone was detected near the Belarus border.

video:video.video:title: North London celebrates as Arsenal wins its first Premier League title in 22 years
video:video.video:description: Celebrations continued into the night in north London on Tuesday after the Arsenal soccer team was crowned Premier League champion.

video:video.video:title: WHO says risk of Ebola spread is high at national levels in Congo and low at global level
video:video.video:description: The head of the World Health Organization said on Wednesday the risk of global spread of the Ebola outbreak in Congo and Uganda is high at national and regional levels but low at the global level.

video:video.video:title: Jensen Ackles talks 'The Boys' spin-off at series finale premiere
video:video.video:description: Actor Jensen Ackles shares his hopes that "The Boys" spin-off "Vought Rising" will be as successful as the hit superhero series, as the cast gathered in Los Angeles to celebrate the final episode of the Prime Video show.

video:video.video:title: Barney Frank, longtime congressman and gay rights pioneer, dies at 86
video:video.video:description: Barney Frank, the longtime Democratic congressman and leading liberal who brought new visibility to gay rights and crafted the most significant reforms to the financial system in a generation, has died.

video:video.video:title: Mexico City airport races to finish facelift ahead of upcoming FIFA World Cup
video:video.video:description: Workers at Mexico City International Airport are scrambling to finish months of disruptive renovations before the 2026 FIFA World Cup kicks off on June 11, as Mexico City prepares to host the tournament’s opening match and an expected surge of international visitors.

video:video.video:title: Democratic lawmakers react after liberal congressman Barney Frank dies
video:video.video:description: Democratic lawmakers react after liberal congressman Barney Frank dies

video:video.video:title: Unexploded drone removed from apartment building in Ukraine
video:video.video:description: Explosives technicians on Wednesday removed an unexploded drone lodged in the facade of a residential high-rise building in Odesa, Ukraine.

video:video.video:title: 3 dead in New Mexico and first responders decontaminated after exposure to unknown substance
video:video.video:description: New Mexico authorities say three people are dead and more than a dozen first responders were assessed for exposure to an unidentified substance after being called to a rural home for a suspected drug overdose.

video:video.video:title: Trump calls it a 'very big day' for Cubans after Raúl Castro indicted
video:video.video:description: President Donald Trump called it a “very big day” after the Justice Department indicted former Cuban leader Raúl Castro in the 1996 downing of civilian exile group planes, while saying he does not believe further escalation against Cuba is necessary.

video:video.video:title: US raises pressure on Cuba with indictment of former leader
video:video.video:description: Prosecutors announced criminal charges against former Cuban President Raúl Castro in the 1996 downing of civilian planes flown by exiles as the Trump administration escalated pressure on the island’s socialist government.

video:video.video:title: Demand soars for Israel's battle-tested weapons tech despite criticism of its wartime conduct
video:video.video:description: When Israeli defense officials approached Massivit last year about using its unique 3D printers to make military drone parts, CEO Yossi Azarzar jumped at the chance.

video:video.video:title: Health workers battle Ebola as normal life continues in Bunia, eastern DR Congo
video:video.video:description: People in the eastern Democratic Republic of Congo city of Bunia appear to be going about life as usual, even amid a rapidly spreading Ebola outbreak.

video:video.video:title: ‘The situation is not yet under control’: Ebola spreads rapidly in eastern Congo
video:video.video:description: In Rwampara, one of the eastern Congolese towns hardest hit by the ongoing Ebola outbreak, healthcare workers in protective gear loaded bodies into coffins in silence on Wednesday.

video:video.video:title: In Iran's capital, an influx of weaponry signals those at home and abroad as war threats remain
video:video.video:description: Weapons are now regularly brandished in Tehran, an increasing show of defiance as U.S. President Donald Trump threatens he could restart the war with Iran should negotiations break down and the Islamic Republic refuses to release its grip on the Strait of Hormuz.

video:video.video:title: Whale detection system uses AI to prevent collisions with ships in San Francisco Bay
video:video.video:description: A new whale-spotting system uses AI to prevent ships from colliding with the giant marine mammals in San Francisco Bay.

video:video.video:title: Holy deception: Rome's 'sexy priest' calendar star never set foot in a seminary
video:video.video:description: A calendar featuring close-ups of young, handsome men in priestly attire has been a perennial Rome souvenir for the last two decades – but few, it seems, are actually men of the cloth.

video:video.video:title: Europe faces stray Ukrainian drones as Kyiv targets Russian oil exports
video:video.video:description: Ukrainian drones recently caused concern by entering Baltic airspace, leading to tensions with NATO countries.

video:video.video:title: Montenegro at 20: After breaking with Serbia and joining NATO, EU is the next frontier
video:video.video:description: Montenegro marks 20 years of independence from a union with Serbia this week, celebrating a two-decade transformation that has already brought the small Balkan country into NATO.

video:video.video:title: US military boards Iranian-flagged oil tanker suspected of trying to breach blockade
video:video.video:description: The U.S. military said Wednesday that it boarded an Iranian-flagged oil tanker in the Gulf of Oman that was suspected of trying to violate the American blockade, the latest action by the Trump administration to try to push Tehran to reopen the Strait of Hormuz.

video:video.video:title: Why meat-eating dinosaurs like T. rex evolved to have tiny arms
video:video.video:description: Why does such a big and powerful dinosaur like the T. rex have such tiny forearms, in a new study scientists believe they might have the answer.

video:video.video:title: This is how Cuba reacted to the US indictment of Raúl Castro
video:video.video:description: Cuban officials reacted angrily on Wednesday after U.S. prosecutors charged former Cuban leader Raúl Castro over the 1996 shootdown of two civilian planes operated by the Miami-based exile group Brothers to the Rescue.

video:video.video:title: A timeline of US-Cuba relations as tensions rise in Trump's second term
video:video.video:description: The U.S. indictment of former Cuban President Raúl Castro is the latest salvo in the Trump administration’s months-long pressure campaign against the Caribbean island's socialist-controlled government.

video:video.video:title: Israeli security minister releases videos taunting detained flotilla activists
video:video.video:description: Israel's national security minister drew a sharp rebuke from his boss and triggered a backlash abroad on Wednesday, after he released videos taunting and telling detained activists from a flotilla that tried to get past the Israeli blockade of Gaza that they should be imprisoned for a very long time

video:video.video:title: Buddhist hall housing 'eternal flame' burns down on Japan's Miyajima Island
video:video.video:description: A Buddhist hall in Western Japan known for housing an "eternal flame" that has been burning for over 1,000 years caught fire and burned to the ground on Miyajima Island.

video:video.video:title: Netanyahu's coalition alliances with religious parties put his reelection at risk
video:video.video:description: Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu has remained in power for the most of the past 17 years due in part to a tight alliance with ultra-Orthodox religious parties.

video:video.video:title: Zookeepers help raise baby aardvark
video:video.video:description: Keepers at the UK's Chester Zoo have welcomed a new arrival - a baby aardvark. The baby, named "Womble" was born to mother Oni on 3 April.

video:video.video:title: AP Top Stories May 21
video:video.video:description: Here’s the latest for Thursday, May 21: Cuba condemns Raul Castro U.S. indictment; U.S. military says it boarded an Iranian-flagged tanker; First responders quarantined after suspected overdose deaths in New Mexico; SpaceX reveals plans for IPO.

video:video.video:title: SpaceX reveals plans for what could be the biggest-ever initial public offering
video:video.video:description: Elon Musk announced plans Wednesday for one of the biggest sales of stock to the public ever for his space company that is currently losing billions of dollars a year.

video:video.video:title: Cantona on soccer and the World Cup
video:video.video:description: As he promotes a documentary about his life, former soccer star Eric Cantona gives his opinion on VAR and explains why he won't be watching the upcoming FIFA World Cup.

video:video.video:title: Son of Mango fashion chain founder posts bail after arrest in Spain over father’s fatal cliff fall
video:video.video:description: The son of Isak Andic, the billionaire founder of the Spanish fashion brand Mango, posted bail of 1 million euros ($1.15 million) on Tuesday after he was charged with homicide in connection with a renewed investigation into the death of his father in 2024.

video:video.video:title: Israeli military strike destroys a house in the Gaza Strip after a warning to leave, residents say
video:video.video:description: A resident in Bureij, central Gaza Strip, said Israel’s military destroyed his four-story house in a post-warning strike.

video:video.video:title: Sharon Stone, Demi Moore bring glamour to Cannes
video:video.video:description: Cannes Film Festival’s crazy red carpet schedule continues - and with it, some stand-out looks from Sharon Stone, Demi Moore and Cate Blanchett. (May 20)

video:video.video:title: What to know about Ebola
video:video.video:description: Here's what to know about Ebola from symptoms to how it spreads.
video:video.video:tag: Centers for Disease Control and Prevention

video:video.video:title: Crazy stunts on 'The Boys'
video:video.video:description: Jensen Ackles and Antony Starr say they didn't go too far over-the-top on "The Boys" when it came to stunts, thanks to showrunner Eric Kripke. (May 20)

video:video.video:title: Auction house sells Matthew Perry personal items and more
video:video.video:description: Matthew Perry’s SAG card and Michael B. Jordan’s “Black Panther” costume are going up for auction. Here’s a preview.

video:video.video:title: Rare baseball and soccer cards available in auction
video:video.video:description: A rare Lionel Messi soccer card and Mickey Mantle rookie baseball card are up for auction. A sports card expert explains the significance of both.

video:video.video:title: Trump orders banks to take a closer look at clients' citizenship in new immigration enforcement move
video:video.video:description: President Donald Trump on Tuesday signed an executive order that requires banks to take a closer look at the citizenship of their customers, a new measure in his administration’s push to clamp down on people living in the country illegally.

video:video.video:title: Jessie T. Usher suffers for craft in 'The Boys'
video:video.video:description: "The Boys" star Jessie T. Usher regales Karen Fukuhara with his most shocking moment on set.

video:video.video:title: Philadelphia residents beat early heat wave at splash pads
video:video.video:description: The eastern U.S. sweated through a second day of early-season heat Wednesday, with some schoolchildren being sent home while others stayed in sweltering classrooms.

video:video.video:title: AP top stories May 20
video:video.video:description: Here's the latest for Wednesday, May 20th: Putin and Xi hail friendship after Trump visit; Officers from Jan. 6 riots sue to block $1.8B fund payouts; Gay rights trailblazer Barney Frank dies; Colorful parade of decorated elephants wows crowds in India.

video:video.video:title: Trump: Netanyahu 'will do whatever I want him to do' on Iran
video:video.video:description: President Donald Trump said Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu “will do whatever I want him to do” on Iran as negotiations continue, a day after saying he was “an hour away” from ordering strikes.

video:video.video:title: Voting rights activists protest redistricting at Mississippi rally
video:video.video:description: Voting rights activists from across the South marched through Jackson, Mississippi, on Wednesday to protest redistricting efforts. (AP Video by Sophie Bates)

video:video.video:title: Xi and Putin highlight their friendship and cooperation on energy and other issues in Beijing visit
video:video.video:description: Chinese leader Xi Jinping and Russian President Vladimir Putin have hailed their strategic ties and energy trade during a meeting in Beijing.

video:video.video:title: AP Top Stories May 20
video:video.video:description: Here’s the latest for Wednesday, May 20th: Authorities say mosque shooting suspects were radicalized online; Russian President Putin meets Chinese President Xi; Republican Rep.

video:video.video:title: Trump says he's postponing signing an executive order on AI
video:video.video:description: President Donald Trump called off a Thursday signing ceremony for a new order on artificial intelligence because he worried it could dull America’s edge on AI technology.

video:video.video:title: Stress relief starts at the scalp: Asia-style head spas gain US fans
video:video.video:description: Head spas, a wellness trend that originated in Japan, are rapidly gaining popularity across the U.S., with hundreds of locations now offering scalp-focused treatments.

video:video.video:title: Trump says he doesn’t 'know' if he’s losing control of Senate Republicans
video:video.video:description: President Donald Trump acknowledged he doesn’t "know” if he’s losing control of Senate Republicans, as GOP lawmakers appeared to abandon $1 billion in security funding for the White House ballroom complex and questions mounted over his administration’s $1.8 billion settlement fund.

video:video.video:title: Federal charges allege $90 million stolen from Minnesota social service programs
video:video.video:description: The new charges against fifteen people were announced as the former leader of a Minnesota nonprofit was sentenced to nearly 42 years in prison for her role in a $250 million fraud case.

video:video.video:title: Baby pygmy hippo takes first outdoor bath at Berlin Zoo
video:video.video:description: A baby pygmy hippopotamus at Berlin Zoo took its first outdoor bath this week after being born on May 9.

video:video.video:title: Heat wave intensifies in India as temperatures soar across northern plains
video:video.video:description: In India, the heat wave has intensified as temperatures soared across the northern plains on Thursday.

video:video.video:title: Firefighters extinguish flames after emergency service says Russian strike hit Sumy
video:video.video:description: Six people were injured in a Russian strike on Ukraine's northeastern city of Sumy on Thursday, the State Emergency Service of Ukraine said.

video:video.video:title: Meet the prehistoric predators that ruled the oceans in Jurassic times
video:video.video:description: A new exhibition at London’s Natural History Museum shines a light on Jurassic marine life and asks what their extinction could teach us about protecting the oceans now.

video:video.video:title: Rome’s ‘sexy priest’ calendar star never set foot in a seminary
video:video.video:description: A calendar featuring close-ups of young, handsome priests has been a popular Rome souvenir for two decades. However, many of those photographed aren't actually priests.

video:video.video:title: US announces criminal case against former Cuban President Raúl Castro
video:video.video:description: The indictment was related to Castro’s alleged role in the shootdown of two small planes operated by the exile group Brothers to the Rescue. (AP video by Cody Jackson)

video:video.video:title: Colorful procession of decorated elephants delights festival-goers in southern India
video:video.video:description: Crowds lined the streets in the southern Indian state of Kerala for a colourful elephant parade. The animals are an integral part of the state's religious traditions and rituals associated with temples and cultural festivities.
video:video.video:tag: People for the Ethical Treatment of Animals

video:video.video:title: NYC Mayor announces affordable World Cup tickets to be made available for some lucky residents
video:video.video:description: New York Mayor Zohran Mamdani announces 1,000 affordable tickets to upcoming World Cup matches to be made available to residents of New York.

video:video.video:title: Shakira opens up about her brightest and darkest moments
video:video.video:description: Shakira reflects on the brightest and darkest moments of her life. She'll perform alongside BTS and Madonna at the World Cup final halftime show in July.

video:video.video:title: 'The Boys' ends: Stars on the spinoff and satire | AP interview
video:video.video:description: After five seasons of death, depravity and digs at the capitalistic superhero-industrial complex, “The Boys” has dropped its series finale.

video:video.video:title: Displaced Lebanese grandmother feeds thousands of compatriots who fled war
video:video.video:description: Soubhiye Zeiter started baking outside her tent in Beirut to feed herself and her family, displaced by war.

video:video.video:title: Fuerzas israelíes interceptan una flotilla que intenta romper el bloqueo a Gaza
video:video.video:description: Las fuerzas armadas israelíes interceptaron botes frente a la costa de Chipre este lunes, como parte de la más reciente ola de flotillas de activistas que intentan romper el bloqueo naval de Israel sobre Gaza.

video:video.video:title: Cannes jury members including Demi Moore attend handprint ceremony
video:video.video:description: Demi Moore and fellow 2026 Cannes Film Festival jury members have made their mark, joining jury president Park Chan-wook at a handprint ceremony.

video:video.video:title: Russia continues large-scale nuclear drills
video:video.video:description: Russia on Thursday released a video showing large-scale maneuvers of its nuclear forces “to attain readiness for combat missions involving missile launches,” its Defense Ministry said.

video:video.video:title: Flotilla activists return to Italy and describe being beaten by Israeli forces
video:video.video:description: Two Italians activists, part of the Gaza Sumud Flotilla arrested by Israeli forces after being intercepted in international waters, returned to Italy on Thursday, describing their detention facility as “a place of terror.”

video:video.video:title: Blackpink's Jisoo appears at Dior event
video:video.video:description: Blackpink's Jisoo appeared before hundreds of fans at a Dior pop-up store event in Hong Kong, showcasing Jonathan Anderson's 2026 Fall collection. (May 21)

video:video.video:title: SpaceX reveals plans for record-breaking IPO that could make Elon Musk a trillionaire
video:video.video:description: Elon Musk announced plans for one of the biggest stock sales ever by taking public a space company that is currently losing billions of dollars a year.

video:video.video:title: 'Love is the New Gangsta' for 6LACK | AP interview
video:video.video:description: For 6LACK, "family is what's cool, being vulnerable is what's cool." The Grammy-nominated rapper and singer-songwriter says "Love is the New Gangsta" represents his most personal — and honest — album to date.

video:video.video:title: 'The Mandalorian and Grogu' | AP interview
video:video.video:description: Pedro Pascal is taking "Baby Yoda" to the big screen in "The Mandalorian and Grogu." The star spoke with AP entertainment reporter Leslie Ambriz at the "Star Wars" film's red carpet premiere about sharing the suit of his helmeted Din Djarin character with stuntmen and body doubles Lateef Crowder and

video:video.video:title: Increased fuel costs have some Americans ready to scale back their Memorial Day travel plans
video:video.video:description: Higher fuel prices and other inflationary pressures are making most forms of travel more expensive as Memorial Day kicks off the summer season in the U.S.

video:video.video:title: Atlantic hurricane season forecast to be milder than normal thanks to El Nino
video:video.video:description: Meteorologists predict a developing El Nino could dampen the upcoming Atlantic hurricane season, but it won't eliminate storms.

video:video.video:title: AP top stories May 21
video:video.video:description: Here's the latest for Thursday, May 21st: Locals set fire to an Ebola treatment center in Congo; Buddhist hall in Japan burns to the ground; Flotilla members return to Italy after Israeli detainment; Temps soar to 113 Fahrenheit in India.

video:video.video:title: Hundreds of flotilla activists land in Turkey, recount abuse and torture by Israeli authorities
video:video.video:description: Wearing grey sweatsuits and Arab keffiyehs, the activists descended the stairs to the runaway in Istanbul flashing two-fingered salutes and chanting “Free Palestine.”

video:video.video:title: Buddhist hall housing 'eternal flame' burns down in Japan
video:video.video:description: A Buddhist hall in Western Japan known for housing an "eternal flame" that has been burning for over 1,000 years caught fire and burned to the ground on Miyajima Island.

video:video.video:title: Tennessee governor grants Tony Carruthers one-year stay after his execution was halted
video:video.video:description: Tennessee’s governor has granted a one-year stay of execution for Tony Carruthers after his lethal injection was halted when officials struggled to find a vein for more than an hour.

video:video.video:title: NYC residents could score $50 World Cup tickets through a new lottery
video:video.video:description: New York City residents will soon get a chance to buy $50 World Cup tickets through a city-run lottery, as officials try to make the tournament more accessible amid soaring ticket prices.

video:video.video:title: Dog rescued after falling off cliff
video:video.video:description: A dog named Bailey was stranded after falling off a cliff top. In challenging conditions, a crew maneuvered a lifeboat as close as possible to the rocks before a crew member carefully entered the water and successfully rescued the dog.

video:video.video:title: $1 million bond set for livestreamer charged for allegedly shooting a man at Tennessee courthouse
video:video.video:description: A Tennessee judge has set a $1 million bond for a white livestreamer charged with attempted murder for allegedly shooting and wounding a Black man.

video:video.video:title: Protest in Athens after Israel intercepts international flotilla carrying Gaza activists
video:video.video:description: More than a thousand people gathered in the streets of Athens on Thursday to protest after Israeli forces intercepted an international flotilla carrying Gaza activists.

video:video.video:title: Trump's Washington arch design gets approval from key federal agency
video:video.video:description: A key federal agency has approved the design for an arch that President Donald Trump wants to build at an entrance to the nation's capital.

video:video.video:title: Saudi Arabia’s Interior Ministry opens its annual Hajj security parade ahead of pilgrimage season
video:video.video:description: Saudi Arabia’s Interior Ministry opens its annual Hajj security parade ahead of pilgrimage season

video:video.video:title: US raises pressure on Cuba with indictment of former leader
video:video.video:description: Federal prosecutors announced criminal charges against former Cuban President Raúl Castro in the 1996 downing of civilian planes flown by Miami-based exiles as the Trump administration escalated pressure on the island’s socialist government.

video:video.video:title: Lizzo promises 'good, clean fun' on new album
video:video.video:description: Lizzo says her new album is "good, clean fun" as she walks the carpet at the starry amfAR gala outside the Cannes Film Festival. (May 21)

video:video.video:title: Ebola cases in eastern Congo’s Rwampara continue to arrive as treatment center is burned
video:video.video:description: People set fire to an Ebola treatment center in a town at the heart of the outbreak in eastern Congo on Thursday after being stopped from retrieving the body of a local man, a witness and a senior police officer said, as fear and anger grow over a health crisis that doctors are struggling to contain

video:video.video:title: Documents show Queen Elizabeth was eager for ex-Prince Andrew to become trade envoy
video:video.video:description: The late Queen Elizabeth II was “very keen” for former Prince Andrew to be named Britain’s trade envoy in 2001, according to documents released Thursday that showed his appointment received little scrutiny from government ministers.

video:video.video:title: Hundreds of Cambodian villagers hold centuries-old ceremony
video:video.video:description: Villagers in Cambodia took part in a centuries-old ritual Thursday, honouring their village’s guardian spirits and praying for good fortune, rain and prosperity.

video:video.video:title: Trump and Putin's meetings with Chinese leader deliver markedly different messages
video:video.video:description: On the surface, Chinese leader Xi Jinping's back-to-back summits with U.S. President Donald Trump and Russia's Vladimir Putin looked pretty similar, with formal handshakes in Beijing's Tiananmen Square, an enthusiastic greeting from flower-waving children, and marching columns of soldiers branching

video:video.video:title: Michigan Rep. Scholten pleased House republicans voted down women's museum bill
video:video.video:description: Hillary Scholten, a House Democrat from Michigan, says she's "pleased" that Republicans in Congress helped strike down a bill to build a Women's Museum after the GOP banned 'biological men' from exhibits.

video:video.video:title: Rep. Gregory Meeks said 'we had the votes' on Iran war resolution before House GOP called session off
video:video.video:description: Democratic Rep. Gregory Meeks said "we had the votes" to compel President Donald Trump to withdraw from the war with Iran before House GOP delayed the session.

video:video.video:title: Rubio says Cuba has ‘always’ been a national security threat to the US
video:video.video:description: U.S. Secretary of State Marco Rubio says Cuba has “always” been a national security threat to the United States.

video:video.video:title: After years of war, Sudan’s capital is left with thousands of unmarked graves
video:video.video:description: In Khartoum state, hastily buried bodies litter the city - buried in homes, in sports fields or by the road. Dr.

video:video.video:title: After years of war, Sudan’s capital is left with thousands of unmarked graves
video:video.video:description: Three years of war have devastated much of Sudan, as The Associated Press' Fay Abuelgasim reports from the city of Omdurman, which sits on the western bank of the River Nile, opposite to the capital Khartoum.

video:video.video:title: Street artist JR begins inflating immersive cave on Pont Neuf bridge in Paris
video:video.video:description: Street artist JR and his team began inflating an immersive cave on the Pont Neuf bridge in Paris in the early hours of Thursday morning, in an exceptionally massive art installation in the ancient heart of the French capital.

video:video.video:title: Sherlock Holmes enthusiasts reenact iconic duel at Switzerland’s Reichenbach Falls
video:video.video:description: From 221B Baker Street in central London to a cliffside waterfall in the Swiss Alps, generations of armchair detectives are celebrating International Sherlock Holmes Day.

video:video.video:title: French artist JR inflates a giant ‘cave’ over Paris’ oldest bridge
video:video.video:description: Paris’ oldest bridge has vanished. This week, the artist known as the “French Banksy,” JR, inflated a giant “cave” over the Pont Neuf — a monumental, rocky illusion swallowing the 17th-century landmark whole.

video:video.video:title: Sudan's war has left thousands missing. Many are buried in unmarked graves
video:video.video:description: It was a classic husband's phone call. He was done for the day and would stop at the market before coming home. But he was returning from war, not work.

video:video.video:title: 2-time NASCAR champion Kyle Busch has died at 41, NASCAR says
video:video.video:description: Kyle Busch, a two-time Cup Series champion who won more races than anyone across NASCAR’s three national series, has died at 41.

video:video.video:title: Rubio says US President Trump is "very disappointed at NATO right now”
video:video.video:description: Secretary of State Marco Rubio is on his latest mission to assuage nervous U.S. allies in Europe about the Trump administration’s intentions with NATO or at least put a friendlier face on whipsawing changes and uncertainty about American troop reductions.

video:video.video:title: Ebola treatment center burns in eastern Congo city of Rwampara
video:video.video:description: The Ebola treatment center in Rwampara in the Democratic Republic of Congo, burned Thursday as gunshots could be heard nearby and local residents were seen chasing away and hitting a white four-wheel drive vehicle often used by NGOs in the area.
